content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

October 22, 1990

Patricia J. Randolph  
Deputy County Counsel  
Administration and Courts Building  
1415 Truxtun Avenue, 5th Floor  
Bakersfield, CA 93301

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-632

Dear Ms. Randolph:

This letter is in response to your request for confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you on September 14, 1990, regarding the filing and disclosure obligations of members of the Board of Retirement of Kern County Employees' Retirement Association under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

Your letter correctly states our advice. Pending adoption of proposed Regulation 18720, which clarifies the term "other public officials who manage public investments" as that term is used in Section 87200, members of the Board of Retirement of Kern County Employees' Retirement Association should continue to file Commission Form 730, Statements of Economic Interests. Should the Commission adopt proposed Regulation 18720 and include members of the board among those public officials who must file Commission Form 721, Statements of Economic Interests, you will be notified.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry. Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:plh
